# {{ system_name }}/{{ stack_name }}/{{ layer_name }} layer

Layer type: iac-terragrunt

This scaffold creates a Terraform module with Terragrunt wrapper for multi-environment infrastructure management.

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-LAYER -->
<!-- This section can be regenerated by automation. -->
<!-- END AUTO-LAYER -->

## Structure

```
{{ layer_name }}/
├── layer.yaml              # Taxonomy node descriptor
├── composite/
│   └── terraform/          # Terraform module
│       ├── main.tf
│       ├── variables.tf
│       ├── outputs.tf
│       └── versions.tf
└── <environment>/          # Per-environment config
    ├── environment.yaml
    └── terragrunt.hcl
```

## Getting Started

1. Copy `env-template/` for each environment:
   ```bash
   cp -r env-template dev
   cp -r env-template staging
   cp -r env-template prod
   ```

2. Update `terragrunt.hcl` with environment-specific values

3. Initialize and apply:
   ```bash
   cd dev
   terragrunt init
   terragrunt plan
   terragrunt apply
   ```

## Terraform Module

The `composite/terraform/` directory contains the reusable Terraform module:

```hcl
# main.tf
resource "aws_example" "this" {
  name        = var.name
  environment = var.environment
}

# variables.tf
variable "name" {
  type        = string
  description = "Resource name"
}

variable "environment" {
  type        = string
  description = "Environment name"
}

# outputs.tf
output "id" {
  value = aws_example.this.id
}
```

## Terragrunt Configuration

Each environment directory contains a `terragrunt.hcl`:

```hcl
# dev/terragrunt.hcl
include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

terraform {
  source = "../composite/terraform"
}

inputs = {
  name        = "{{ layer_name }}"
  environment = "dev"
  region      = "us-east-1"
}
```

## Common Commands

```bash
# Initialize
terragrunt init

# Plan changes
terragrunt plan

# Apply changes
terragrunt apply

# Destroy resources
terragrunt destroy

# Apply across all environments
terragrunt run-all apply
```

## State Management

Terragrunt manages remote state automatically. Configure your backend in the root `terragrunt.hcl`:

```hcl
remote_state {
  backend = "s3"
  config = {
    bucket         = "my-terraform-state"
    key            = "${path_relative_to_include()}/terraform.tfstate"
    region         = "us-east-1"
    encrypt        = true
    dynamodb_table = "terraform-locks"
  }
}
```

## CI/CD Integration

```yaml
# GitHub Actions example
- name: Terragrunt Plan
  working-directory: {{ layer_name }}/dev
  run: terragrunt plan -out=tfplan

- name: Terragrunt Apply
  working-directory: {{ layer_name }}/dev
  run: terragrunt apply tfplan
```

## Best Practices

- **DRY Configuration**: Use `include` blocks to share common configuration
- **Environment Isolation**: Separate state per environment/region
- **Locking**: Always use a lock table (DynamoDB for AWS)
- **Plan Before Apply**: Review plans in CI before applying
